title: NASA's twin Voyager spacecraft are very low on power after nearly 50 years. How long can they keep going?
summary: NASA's Voyager spacecraft, launched in 1977, are running low on power after nearly 50 years in interstellar space. It remains uncertain how much longer they can continue operating.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 commander and astrophotographer team up to capture breathtaking, never-before-seen shots of the moon's far side
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman and astrophotographer Andrew McCarthy are collaborating to capture unprecedented images of the moon's far side. This partnership aims to produce breathtaking visuals of a rarely seen lunar region.

title: Pentagon unveils trove of declassfied 'UFO' videos. How to see them all, from 'a football-shaped body' to 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light'
summary: The Pentagon released nearly 30 declassified UFO videos, including footage of objects described as a 'football-shaped body' and a 'misshapen and uneven ball of white light'. The documents were unveiled on May 8 as part of a new tranche of UFO files.

title: Black holes slamming into scorching stars may be causing mysterious blue flashes in the cosmos
summary: Scientists propose that Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients (LFBOTs) may result from black holes or neutron stars colliding with the universe's hottest stars. These blue cosmic explosions are among the brightest and most enigmatic phenomena in the cosmos.

title: Monopoly sucks, but this new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff might add some galactic sizzle
summary: A new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff is generating excitement in the gaming community. The article humorously references the concept of monopolies while highlighting the game's potential appeal.

title: Blue Origin tests 1st moon lander ahead of lunar launch later this year (photo)
summary: Blue Origin has tested its first moon lander at NASA centers in preparation for a lunar mission later this year. The company is advancing its spacecraft for upcoming lunar exploration efforts.

title: 'Star Fox' is returning this summer with a shiny 'Star Fox 64' remake for Nintendo Switch 2 full of unique features
summary: Nintendo announced a remake of 'Star Fox 64' titled 'Star Fox' for the upcoming Nintendo Switch 2, featuring unique features and a return to the Lylat Wars. The game is set to release this summer.

title: The charred hull of Artemis 2's Orion | Space photo of the day for May 8, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is shown resting after its lunar mission, with a charred hull from re-entry. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day on May 8, 2026.

title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch for their 34th Commercial Resupply Mission to the International Space Station. The Dragon spacecraft will carry about 6,500 pounds of supplies and equipment aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a colorized image of Mars during a gravity assist approach on May 3, 2026. The spacecraft is set to perform a gravity assist on May 15 to adjust its trajectory toward asteroid Psyche.

title: I Am Artemis: Anton Kiriwas
summary: Anton Kiriwas, a senior technical integration manager for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, shares his journey and inspiration from seeing images of the Moon and Mars at a job fair. His story reflects the challenges and aspirations of working in space exploration.

title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
summary: NASA has advanced high-performance spaceflight computing, starting with the Apollo Guidance Computers in the 1960s. The agency continues to develop radiation-hardened processors for mission-critical spacecraft functions.

title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a stunning image of the Milky Way rising above Earth's atmospheric glow from the International Space Station aboard a SpaceX Dragon. The phenomenon, known as airglow, is caused by excited atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere releasing light.

title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
summary: NASA appointed Brian Hughes as senior director of launch operations at Kennedy Space Center. He will oversee launch infrastructure and report to NASA Headquarters.

title: NASA Fuel Cell Tests Pave Way for Energy Storage on Moon
summary: NASA conducted fuel cell tests to advance energy storage solutions for lunar missions. The cylindrical fuel cell system, equipped with numerous sensors and tubes, was carefully installed for evaluation.

title: Tracy Arm’s Post-Tsunami Landscape
summary: A landslide-triggered tsunami in summer 2025 stripped vegetation from the shore of Tracy Arm, a glacial fjord. The event highlights the impact of natural disasters on remote glacial landscapes.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 supersonic jets and a Pilatus PC-12 utility plane, continuing its legacy in flight research and experimental missions. The center, based in Edwards, California, supports science and aeronautics test missions, honoring pioneers like Neil Armstrong.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords during a ceremony in Asunción, joining the international coalition for civil space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Paraguay's commitment to shared principles in space exploration.

title: Booster 19 completes Static Fire as Ship 39 prepares for rollout
summary: Booster 19 successfully completed a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026. Ship 39 is now preparing for rollout as its partner.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making strides toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is being converted for future missions, with Flight 12 approaching after six months since Flight 11 concluded. The upgrade work is part of ongoing preparations for upcoming launches.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This may precede a busier period in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The company has issued notices for the upcoming launch, signaling a potential return to flight operations.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, experienced in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut's background highlights their readiness for the challenges of spaceflight.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant milestone for the company's heavy-lift capabilities.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in the space sector.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a spacecraft or satellite's design or capabilities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for spacecraft parachutes, referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out,' likely involving thermal testing to ensure functionality in extreme conditions. This test is essential for validating the safety and reliability of parachutes used in space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation could significantly improve space operations and debris management.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. This event allows small and medium-sized enterprises to provide feedback on space sector topics.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste achieved its first navigation signal, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was strategically chosen.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round, unveiling a new rover. The funding reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for a sustained US presence on the Moon.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, rebranding it as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company's shares were priced at the top of the $24-$26 range outlined in its May 1 S-1 filing.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging satellites as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to fill gaps in current EO capabilities with its advanced thermal imaging technology.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is advancing efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country seeks to capitalize on the growing importance of quantum technologies in various industries.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy. This approach aims to mitigate vulnerabilities associated with fuel line disruptions and deliveries.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis has raised over $450 million in a funding round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion. This Series E round highlights growing investor confidence in the space industry.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation, aiming to scale its business and meet increasing demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to simplify the regulatory process for new space applications.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. Planet Labs Germany secured the deal, marking a significant contract in the European space sector.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, facing challenges due to data limitations. The shift highlights the need for scalable solutions to support continuous AI-driven satellite operations.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, coinciding with its acquisition process by Amazon. The satellite operator's financial performance highlights its position as a key player in the space industry.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026, driven by progress on its Neutron launch vehicle, a robotics acquisition, and an SBI deal. The company highlighted strong momentum ahead of Neutron's upcoming launch.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, has secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The company is a rising star in India's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after securing high-end pricing.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has inaugurated a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with an annual production capacity of up to 100 satellites. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported a 40% year-over-year growth in its satellite manufacturing revenue for Q1 2026, driven by projects like Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley stated that the Globalstar deal did not impact the company's financial performance.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, adapt to evolving threats, and modernize its workforce. The agency's new director emphasizes resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.
